plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to reduce risk.
Trim income (QQQI) and broad index (SPY) to free up capital.
Add new, strong names (RTX, XLV) for diversification and stability.
Focus on capital preservation with a larger cash buffer.
Maintain tight stops on all positions.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdowns but also capping upside potential.

plan: Reduce exposure to leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and exit others (SPAL).
Trim broad index (SPY, QQQ) positions to lower concentration.
Add new, high-scoring names (RTX, XLV) for balanced growth.
Aim for risk-first growth with a moderate cash buffer.
Keep total new additions within trimmed amounts plus buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly return by blending broad market exposure with quality names and minimal leverage. It aims for steady growth while actively managing risk and concentration.

moves.note: Add high R, strong setup name

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and consider others (SPAL).
Trim broad index exposure (SPY, QQQ) to concentrate capital.
Add high-scoring, high-reward names (RTX, PLTR, SNOW) aggressively.
Maintain a small cash buffer, leaning into leverage and concentration.
Use wider but strict stops to manage amplified risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly return by embracing leverage and concentration in top-rated setups. It significantly increases upside potential but also amplifies drawdowns and leverage decay risk, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (SMH, CRWD).
Significantly increase allocation to income-focused QQQI.
Maintain a substantial position in the broad index (SPY) for stability.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Keep a significant cash buffer for yield and safety.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly return through steady distributions and capital preservation. It minimizes volatility by exiting leveraged and high-growth names, focusing on yield-generating assets and broad market stability.
